title: His brother passed away from glioblastoma. He’s running Boston for cancer research
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Colin Gerner is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support brain cancer research in memory of his brother, GJ.
Gerner co-founded StacheStrong, raising millions for brain cancer research and launching a $1,000,000 grant for Massachusetts General Hospital.
The grant, in collaboration with the V Foundation, aims to improve glioblastoma treatments and understanding at Massachusetts General Hospital.

title: Rapper Flavor Flav donates sports equipment to Cambridge youth center
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Rapper Flavor Flav donated sports equipment to Frisoli Youth Center in Cambridge, enhancing after-school and evening programs.
The donation included basketballs, dodgeballs, volleyballs, soccer equipment, tennis balls, kickballs, and cornhole gear.
Frisoli Youth Center, one of five city-run centers, supports youths in physical activity and community engagement.

title: Jaylen Brown has a candid answer when asked if he deserves NBA MVP this season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown is averaging 28.8 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 5.2 assists per game for the Boston Celtics this season.
Brown candidly stated that he may not be MVP analytically, but emphasized his leadership and determination this season.
FanDuel Sportsbook lists Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, Victor Wembanyama, and Nikola Jokic as favorites over Brown for the NBA MVP award.

title: Special World Cup ticket sales shatter Commuter Rail records in first day
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Over 17,450 round-trip World Cup train tickets were sold on the first day, breaking Commuter Rail records.
Nearly 11,000 of these tickets were sold in just the first hour on Wednesday, the MBTA reported.
The MBTA plans to run 14 trains per match, expecting to transport up to 20,000 riders per event.

title: The Boston Fleet are ready to take a sold-out TD Garden by storm
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Fleet will play their first sold-out game at TD Garden against Montreal Victoire on Saturday.
The Fleet feature Olympic gold medalists Megan Keller, Aerin Frankel, and Haley Winn, contributing to their high profile.
The PWHL has seen significant growth, recently setting a record with 18,006 fans at Madison Square Garden.

title: Fall River woman who led sex trafficking ring with 5 others pleads guilty
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Christy Parker, 28, of Fall River, has pleaded guilty to leading a 2023 interstate sex trafficking ring involving minors.
Parker faces charges including sex trafficking by force and coercion, with potential sentences of 10 years to life.
Five of the six indicted in July 2024 have pleaded guilty; one remains to face trial, according to prosecutors.

title: Police look to identify owners of 2 dogs found dead on South Shore
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Police in Weymouth are investigating the deaths of two dogs found at Whitmans Pond and Lakeshore Road.
A terrier-type dog was found dead with no ID but a flea collar; its death wasn't deemed suspicious.
A Pitbull mix was found in a plastic bag; its death is considered suspicious, prompting a public appeal for information.

title: She’s running Boston in memory of her son, and for  necrotizing enterocolitis
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jennifer Canvasser will run the 2026 Boston Marathon in memory of her son, Micah, and to support the NEC Society.
Canvasser founded the NEC Society after losing Micah to necrotizing enterocolitis, aiming to raise awareness and prevent the disease.
Following the marathon, Canvasser will present at the Pediatric Academic Societies Meeting, leading sessions on post-diagnosis NEC care.

title: Braintree man sentenced to life in prison for murdering Salem State student 40 years ago
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: John Carey, 66, sentenced to life for 1986 murder of Salem State student Claire Gravel in Braintree.
Carey was indicted in 2022 after DNA evidence linked him to Gravel's murder, found guilty in March 2026.
Gravel's body was found on Route 128, case went cold until a lead and DNA breakthrough emerged in 2012.

title: ‘It’s pure excitement’: What James Hagens said after first practice with Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens, 19, joined the Boston Bruins for his first practice, emphasizing focus on acclimating to the NHL environment.
Head coach Marco Sturm is cautious about debuting Hagens, considering the young forward's readiness and team needs.
Hagens aims to boost the Bruins' offense, potentially impacting their power play efficiency and third-line scoring capabilities.
